Source URL: https://www.score.org/what-to-expect/

# BOSS Checklist for Preparing a User for a First SCORE Session

Last reviewed for BOSS Library ingestion: 2026-06-21

## BOSS Library tags
score, boss-process, first-session, checklist

## Resource purpose
Checklist BOSS can give users before their first SCORE mentoring session.

## How the BOSS AI Librarian should use this resource
Use after recommending a mentor, especially when the user may not know what to bring or how to use the session.

## Best-fit BOSS lifecycle stage
All stages.

## Key resources or subresources
- Write a one-paragraph business idea or business description.
- List the top 1-3 decisions/questions for the mentor.
- Identify the current business stage.
- Summarize what has already been done: sales, registrations, spending, customers, research, equipment, website, partners.
- Bring any rough numbers: startup costs, expected price, expected customers, current sales, monthly expenses, debt, or cash flow concerns.
- Bring any worksheets already completed: Business Model Canvas, startup budget, projections, target market worksheet, or business plan draft.
- Ask the mentor which SCORE templates, workshops, or additional mentors are most relevant next.
- End the session with one or two clear next actions.

## BOSS routing notes
- This checklist reduces overwhelm and improves mentor matching/outcomes.
- For early-stage users, ask them not to spend heavily on branding, websites, equipment, or entity setup until they have clarified the concept and validation path with the mentor.

## Response pattern for BOSS
Use this resource as a routing destination, not as a substitute for professional legal, tax, lending, procurement, or compliance advice. For time-sensitive workshops, deadlines, prices, contact details, or eligibility rules, send the user to the live source URL first.
